import * as React from "react"
import * as SliderPrimitive from "@radix-ui/react-slider"

import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"

const Slider = React.forwardRef<
  React.ElementRef<typeof SliderPrimitive.Root>,
  React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof SliderPrimitive.Root>
>(({ className, ...props }, ref) => (
  <SliderPrimitive.Root
    ref={ref}
    className={cn(
      "relative flex w-full touch-none select-none items-center",
      className
    )}
    {...props}
  >
    <SliderPrimitive.Track className="relative h-2 w-full grow overflow-hidden rounded-full bg-secondary">
      <SliderPrimitive.Range className="absolute h-full bg-primary" />
    </SliderPrimitive.Track>
    <SliderPrimitive.Thumb className="block h-5 w-5 rounded-full border-2 border-primary bg-background ring-offset-background transition-colors focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2 disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50" />
  </SliderPrimitive.Root>
))
Slider.displayName = SliderPrimitive.Root.displayName

export { Slider }

import * as React from "react"
import * as CheckboxPrimitive from "@radix-ui/react-checkbox"
import { Check } from "lucide-react"

import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"

const Checkbox = React.forwardRef<
  React.ElementRef<typeof CheckboxPrimitive.Root>,
  React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof CheckboxPrimitive.Root>
>(({ className, ...props }, ref) => (
  <CheckboxPrimitive.Root
    ref={ref}
    className={cn(
      "peer h-4 w-4 shrink-0 rounded-sm border border-primary ring-offset-background focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2 disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50 data-[state=checked]:bg-primary data-[state=checked]:text-primary-foreground",
      className
    )}
    {...props}
  >
    <CheckboxPrimitive.Indicator
      className={cn("flex items-center justify-center text-current")}
    >
      <Check className="h-4 w-4" />
    </CheckboxPrimitive.Indicator>
  </CheckboxPrimitive.Root>
))
Checkbox.displayName = CheckboxPrimitive.Root.displayName

export { Checkbox }

client/src/components/ui/scroll-area.tsx.content:
import * as React from "react"
import * as ScrollAreaPrimitive from "@radix-ui/react-scroll-area"

import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"

const ScrollArea = React.forwardRef<
  React.ElementRef<typeof ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root>,
  React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root>
>(({ className, children, ...props }, ref) => (
  <ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root
    ref={ref}
    className={cn("relative overflow-hidden", className)}
    {...props}
  >
    <ScrollAreaPrimitive.Viewport className="h-full w-full rounded-[inherit]">
      {children}
    </ScrollAreaPrimitive.Viewport>
    <ScrollBar />
    <ScrollAreaPrimitive.Corner />
  </ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root>
))
ScrollArea.displayName = ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root.displayName

const ScrollBar = React.forwardRef<
  React.ElementRef<typeof ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaScrollbar>,
  React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaScrollbar>
>(({ className, orientation = "vertical", ...props }, ref) => (
  <ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaScrollbar
    ref={ref}
    orientation={orientation}
    className={cn(
      "flex touch-none select-none transition-colors",
      orientation === "vertical" &&
        "h-full w-2.5 border-l border-l-transparent p-[1px]",
      orientation === "horizontal" &&
        "h-2.5 flex-col border-t border-t-transparent p-[1px]",
      className
    )}
    {...props}
  >
    <ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaThumb className="relative flex-1 rounded-full bg-border" />
  </ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaScrollbar>
))
ScrollBar.displayName = ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaScrollbar.displayName

export { ScrollArea, ScrollBar }

shared/profile-schema.ts.content:
import { pgTable, text, varchar, timestamp, boolean, integer } from "drizzle-orm/pg-core";
import { createInsertSchema } from "drizzle-zod";
import { z } from "zod";

export const userProfiles = pgTable("user_profiles", {
  id: integer("id").primaryKey().generatedByDefaultAsIdentity(),
  walletAddress: varchar("wallet_address", { length: 42 }).notNull().unique(),
  displayName: varchar("display_name", { length: 100 }),
  firstName: varchar("first_name", { length: 50 }),
  lastName: varchar("last_name", { length: 50 }),
  email: varchar("email", { length: 255 }),
  phoneNumber: varchar("phone_number", { length: 20 }),
  companyName: varchar("company_name", { length: 200 }),
  jobTitle: varchar("job_title", { length: 100 }),
  country: varchar("country", { length: 100 }),
  city: varchar("city", { length: 100 }),
  address: text("address"),
  postalCode: varchar("postal_code", { length: 20 }),
  dateOfBirth: varchar("date_of_birth", { length: 10 }), // YYYY-MM-DD format
  nationality: varchar("nationality", { length: 100 }),
  idType: varchar("id_type", { length: 50 }), // passport, driver_license, national_id
  idNumber: varchar("id_number", { length: 100 }),
  taxId: varchar("tax_id", { length: 100 }),
  website: varchar("website", { length: 255 }),
  linkedIn: varchar("linkedin", { length: 255 }),
  twitter: varchar("twitter", { length: 255 }),
  bio: text("bio"),
  avatar: text("avatar"), // base64 image data or URL
  kycStatus: varchar("kyc_status", { length: 20 }).default("pending"), // pending, verified, rejected
  kycDocuments: text("kyc_documents"), // JSON array of document hashes
  isPublic: boolean("is_public").default(false),
  lastUpdated: timestamp("last_updated").defaultNow(),
  createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow(),
});

export const insertUserProfileSchema = createInsertSchema(userProfiles).omit({
  id: true,
  createdAt: true,
  lastUpdated: true,
});

export type UserProfile = typeof userProfiles.$inferSelect;
export type InsertUserProfile = z.infer<typeof insertUserProfileSchema>;

"use client"

import * as React from "react"
import * as ProgressPrimitive from "@radix-ui/react-progress"

import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"

const Progress = React.forwardRef<
  React.ElementRef<typeof ProgressPrimitive.Root>,
  React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof ProgressPrimitive.Root>
>(({ className, value, ...props }, ref) => (
  <ProgressPrimitive.Root
    ref={ref}
    className={cn(
      "relative h-4 w-full overflow-hidden rounded-full bg-secondary",
      className
    )}
    {...props}
  >
    <ProgressPrimitive.Indicator
      className="h-full w-full flex-1 bg-primary transition-all"
      style={{ transform: `translateX(-${100 - (value || 0)}%)` }}
    />
  </ProgressPrimitive.Root>
))
Progress.displayName = ProgressPrimitive.Root.displayName

export { Progress }

replit.md.content:
# Overview

This project is a blockchain communication and trade finance platform designed to facilitate secure financial interactions across multiple networks. It integrates wallet management, real-time messaging, and a smart contract-based escrow system for trade finance. The platform aims to decentralize traditional trade finance processes, offer wallet-to-wallet voice/video communication, and establish a robust rewards system. The business vision includes building a professional Web3 marketing presence and securing significant funding, targeting a $3.3 trillion digital asset market and the $4 trillion trade finance gap with a mainnet launch on Base network in July 2026.

# User Preferences

Preferred communication style: Simple, everyday language.

# System Architecture

## Core Design
The platform utilizes a multi-network architecture with a React 18, TypeScript, and Vite frontend, an Express.js and WebSockets backend, and PostgreSQL with Drizzle ORM on Neon for the database. Blockchain integration uses ethers.js, supporting multi-network operations on Base Sepolia and leveraging the EIP-2535 Diamond Standard for modular smart contracts.

## UI/UX
The frontend is built with shadcn/ui components (based on Radix UI) and Tailwind CSS for styling. State management uses TanStack Query for server state and React hooks for local state, with Wouter for routing and Framer Motion for animations.

## Technical Implementations
- **Wallet Management**: Supports main and contract-specific sub-wallets with AES-256 encrypted private keys, session-based access, automated sub-wallet generation, and real-time balance tracking.
- **Real-time Communication**: WebSocket-based messaging with delivery status and file attachments. WebRTC enables secure, peer-to-peer voice and video calls between wallet addresses.
- **Smart Contract Platform**: An escrow system facilitates secure trade finance with milestone payments, multi-party agreements, and dispute resolution, utilizing the EIP-2535 Diamond Standard for upgradeability.
- **Security**: Features include encrypted private key storage, session-based authentication, automatic wallet locking, secure transaction signing, input validation, SQL injection prevention, and secure WebSocket protocols. Cryptographic signature verification for trade lifecycle API routes is a critical future enhancement for production.
- **Trade Finance Portal (MVP)**: Implements a Pool Guarantee system for blockchain-based trade finance, replacing traditional Letter of Credit bank guarantees. It features a dual-tab interface, role-based workflow (Buyer/Seller), and a simplified horizontal 8-step progress tracker showing complete workflow: Applied → Draft Sent → Seller Approved → Fee Paid → Cert Issued → Goods Shipped → Delivery Confirmed → Payment Complete. The progress tracker enforces strict sequential completion - no step can be marked complete unless all previous steps are completed (no jumping allowed). Sellers can upload shipment proof (Bill of Lading) after certificate issuance (when `tradeLifecycleStatus = 'guarantee_issued'`). Upon upload, the status changes to `bol_in_treasury_custody`, triggering the buyer's "Confirm Delivery" button. Sellers can file default claims if buyers fail to pay after delivery confirmation. Treasury guarantees 80% of invoice value, with the seller bearing 20% risk. A Bill of Lading custody system ensures collateral control. The system includes an enhanced ICC URDG 758 compliant certificate generation with PDF export functionality. **Partial Payment Support**: Invoices can be settled in multiple installments - the system tracks cumulative payments in `totalAmountPaid`, displays remaining balance, allows custom payment amounts, and automatically hides the settlement button when fully paid. The "Settle Invoice" button changes to "Pay Remaining" after partial payments are made.
- **Treasury Portal**: A separate product accessible via `/treasury`, managing staking and voting on Pool Guarantee applications. **Dynamic Co-Staking Model**: Users must stake both USDC AND BFX to participate. Ratio evolves: 50/50 at launch → 45/55 (Growth) → 40/60 (Mature). **40% USDC floor is permanent** for trade finance stability. **Fee Distribution (40/30/20/10)**: 40% USDC to stakers, 30% used to buy and burn BFX (deflationary), 20% BFX rewards to stakers, 10% treasury operations. Features include dynamic voting power display, manual draft certificate creation for approved applications, earnings tracker, advanced search/filters, platform analytics, notification system, claim voting system for buyer defaults, and automatic USDC payment system for approved claims. **Slashing Waterfall**: On defaults, BFX is slashed first, then USDC, then treasury covers remaining (up to 80% guarantee). **Seller Notifications**: When treasury reaches 60% approval threshold, sellers automatically receive notifications about the approval and draft certificate issuance.
- **Professional Specialist Registry**: A comprehensive system for Trade Finance Specialists and Fraud Investigators accessible via `/treasury/specialists`. Features include specialist role registration with professional credentials upload, verification badge system, performance statistics tracking (reviews completed, approval rates, response times), specialization tagging (URDG 758, Letters of Credit, Document Verification, etc.), credential management with document attachments, search and filtering capabilities, and role-based separation distinguishing Trade Finance Specialists from Fraud Investigators. Anyone can stake USDC for voting power, but specialists showcase verified expertise through professional credentials and track records to build trust within the ecosystem.
- **Vote Delegation System**: Enables liquid democracy where stakers can delegate their voting power to trusted Trade Finance Specialists or Fraud Investigators. Stakers retain their staked USDC + BFX and continue earning fee distributions (40% USDC + 20% BFX), while specialists vote on their behalf using combined voting power (personal stake + delegated votes). Features include one-click delegation from specialist profiles, delegation management dashboard showing active delegates, ability to revoke delegation at any time, and real-time delegation statistics on specialist cards (total delegated power, number of delegators). Voting power calculation: Personal Votes (1 USDC = 1 vote) + Delegated Votes from all delegators. This creates accountability as specialists must maintain good decision-making records to retain delegated power.
- **B2B Trade Partner Marketplace**: A comprehensive marketplace accessible via `/marketplace` for discovering reliable trading partners in high-value commodity trades (oil, sugar, agriculture), with a focus on Africa trade corridors targeting the $1.2T market. Features include:
  - **Business Discovery**: Search and filter businesses by industry (Oil & Gas, Agriculture & Food, Mining, etc.), region, and verification status
  - **Business Profiles**: Companies can register with detailed information including industry, country, description, website, and verification badge system
  - **Product Listings**: Businesses can list products with categories, descriptions, pricing, and minimum order quantities
  - **RFQ System**: Request for Quotation workflow where buyers post RFQs with product requirements, delivery dates, and quantities; sellers respond with quotes
  - **Trade Corridor Analytics**: Visualizes Africa-focused trade routes with Pool Guarantee support, showing trade volumes and active traders
  - **Pool Guarantee Integration**: Seamless CTA to access trade financing via "Get Pool Guarantee Financing" buttons linking to the Treasury portal
  - **Review & Reputation System**: Rating system (1-5 stars) for completed trades to build trust and transparency
  - **Business Connections**: Network management for establishing and tracking trade partner relationships
  - **Revenue Model**: 0.1% marketplace fee + 1% Pool Guarantee fee
  - **Risk Strategy**: Ultra-conservative approval for first 100 guarantees to avoid early default death spiral (>5% default rate = catastrophic)

# External Dependencies

## Blockchain Infrastructure
- **Ethers.js**: For blockchain interactions.
- **Multi-Network Support**: Users can switch between networks via dropdown in wallet.
  - **Base Sepolia** (Chain ID: 84532): Primary testnet, RPC: https://sepolia.base.org
    - Diamond Contract: `0x2813e4a2Bd8d59A75db298a31e2b71191214F203`
  - **Lisk Sepolia** (Chain ID: 4202): Secondary testnet, RPC: https://rpc.sepolia-api.lisk.com
    - Diamond Contract: `0x3eDfA00a1E3C158A591097de2FA1756aCD66860D`
    - Deployed: December 11, 2025
- **Network Switcher**: Located in wallet header, persists user preference.
- **Multiple RPC Providers**: Fallback providers for each network ensure reliability.
- **Deployer Address**: `0xef5Bed7c221c85A2c88e3c0223ee45482d6F037d`

## Database Services
- **Neon Database**: Serverless PostgreSQL.
- **Drizzle ORM**: For type-safe database operations.

## Frontend Libraries
- **Radix UI**: Accessible UI components.
- **TanStack Query**: For server state management.
- **Wouter**: For client-side routing.
- **Framer Motion**: For animations.

## Development Tools
- **Vite**: Build tool.
- **TypeScript**: For type safety.
- **Tailwind CSS**: CSS framework.

## Cryptography and Security
- **crypto-js**: For client-side encryption.
- **WebSocket**: For real-time communication.

## Third-party Integrations
- **Coinbase SDK**: For wallet functionality.
- **USDC Integration**: For stablecoin operations.

## File Handling
- **Base64 encoding**: For file attachments.
- **Dropzone**: For file upload interface.

contracts/README.md.content:
# BlockFinaX Smart Contracts (EIP-2535 Diamond Standard)

## Overview

BlockFinaX is a decentralized trade finance platform built on the **EIP-2535 Diamond Standard**, enabling modular, upgradeable smart contracts. The platform replaces traditional Letter of Credit (LC/DLC) bank guarantees with blockchain-based governance where liquidity providers (LPs) stake USDC tokens and vote on trade finance requests.

## Architecture: EIP-2535 Diamond Standard

### Why Diamond Standard?

✅ **Modular & Upgradeable** - Add/remove/replace functionality without redeploying  
✅ **Gas Efficient** - Shared storage across facets reduces deployment costs  
✅ **Unlimited Size** - Bypass Ethereum's 24KB contract size limit  
✅ **Professional Grade** - Industry standard for enterprise DeFi applications  
✅ **Grant-Ready** - Demonstrates advanced smart contract engineering

### Diamond Architecture

```
Diamond.sol (Main Proxy Contract)
    ├── DiamondCutFacet (Upgrade Management)
    ├── DiamondLoupeFacet (Contract Introspection)
    ├── LiquidityPoolFacet (Staking & LP Management)
    ├── GovernanceFacet (Voting & Trade Finance)
    ├── EscrowFacet (Multi-Party Escrow & Milestones)
    ├── InvoiceFacet (On-Chain Invoice Management)
    └── DocumentFacet (Document Hash Verification)
    
LibDiamond.sol (Diamond Storage & Logic)
LibAppStorage.sol (Application Storage)
```

## Smart Contracts

### 1. Diamond.sol (Main Contract)
**Purpose:** Central proxy contract that delegates calls to facets

**Features:**
- Initializes with USDC token address
- Sets minimum stake (100 USDC) and approval threshold (60%)
- Fallback function routes calls to appropriate facets
- Upgradeable via DiamondCut

### 2. DiamondCutFacet
**Purpose:** Manage diamond upgrades (owner only)

**Functions:**
- `diamondCut()` - Add, replace, or remove facets

### 3. DiamondLoupeFacet
**Purpose:** Diamond introspection and transparency

**Functions:**
- `facets()` - Get all facets and their selectors
- `facetFunctionSelectors()` - Get function selectors for a facet
- `facetAddresses()` - Get all facet addresses
- `facetAddress()` - Get facet for a function selector

### 4. LiquidityPoolFacet
**Purpose:** LP staking and pool management

**Functions:**
- `stake(uint256 amount)` - Stake USDC (min 100 USDC)
- `unstake()` - Withdraw staked tokens
- `getStake(address)` - View stake details
- `getPoolStats()` - Total staked, LPs, balance
- `getStakers()` - All LP addresses
- `distributeRewards()` - Send rewards (owner only)

**Features:**
- Automated voting power calculation
- Proportional stake-based voting
- ReentrancyGuard protection
- SafeERC20 transfers

### 5. GovernanceFacet
**Purpose:** Trade finance requests and LP voting

**Functions:**
- `createRequest()` - Submit trade finance request
- `vote(string requestId, bool support)` - Vote on requests
- `releaseFunds()` - Transfer funds to seller after approval
- `getRequest()` - View request details
- `getGovernanceStats()` - Governance statistics

**Features:**
- 60% approval threshold
- One vote per LP per request
- Automated status updates (Pending → Approved/Rejected)
- Funds release to seller after approval

### 6. EscrowFacet
**Purpose:** Multi-party escrow with milestone payments and dispute resolution

**Functions:**
- `createEscrow()` - Create escrow with importer, exporter, arbitrator, and milestones
- `addSubWallet()` - Add sub-wallet with role and permissions
- `completeMilestone()` - Exporter marks milestone as completed
- `releaseMilestonePayment()` - Importer releases payment after milestone completion
- `raiseDispute()` - Either party can raise a dispute
- `resolveDispute()` - Arbitrator resolves disputes and distributes funds
- `getEscrowDetails()` - View escrow information
- `getMilestones()` - Get all milestones
- `getSubWallets()` - View sub-wallet addresses
- `hasPermission()` - Check sub-wallet permissions
- `getEscrowStats()` - Total, active, and completed escrows

**Features:**
- Multi-party support (importer, exporter, arbitrator)
- Milestone-based payment release
- Sub-wallet system with role-based permissions
- Dispute resolution mechanism
- Support for ETH and ERC20 tokens
- Automated arbitrator fee distribution

**Statuses:**
- Escrow: Created → Funded → InProgress → Completed/Disputed
- Milestone: Pending → Completed → Released
- Dispute: None → Raised → InArbitration → Resolved

### 7. InvoiceFacet
**Purpose:** On-chain invoice management with payment verification

**Functions:**
- `createInvoice()` - Create invoice with payer, amount, token, and due date
- `payInvoice()` - Pay invoice with ETH or tokens
- `markInvoiceViewed()` - Payer marks invoice as viewed
- `cancelInvoice()` - Payee cancels unpaid invoice
- `getInvoice()` - View invoice details
- `getInvoiceByNumber()` - Get invoice ID by number
- `isOverdue()` - Check if invoice is past due date
- `getInvoiceStats()` - Total, paid, and pending invoices

**Features:**
- Unique invoice numbering system
- Support for ETH and ERC20 token payments
- On-chain payment verification
- Invoice status tracking (Draft → Sent → Viewed → Paid)
- Overdue detection
- Cancellation protection (cannot cancel paid invoices)

**Statuses:**
- Draft → Sent → Viewed → Paid/Overdue/Cancelled

### 8. DocumentFacet
**Purpose:** Document hash storage and verification for immutable proof

**Functions:**
- `registerDocument()` - Store document hash on-chain with metadata
- `verifyDocument()` - Check if document exists and get uploader/timestamp
- `linkDocumentToEscrow()` - Associate document with escrow contract
- `linkDocumentToInvoice()` - Associate document with invoice
- `markDocumentVerified()` - Mark document as verified
- `getDocument()` - View complete document information
- `getUserDocuments()` - Get all documents uploaded by user
- `getEscrowDocuments()` - Get all documents linked to escrow
- `getInvoiceDocuments()` - Get all documents linked to invoice
- `getTotalDocuments()` - Get total registered documents
- `batchVerifyDocuments()` - Verify multiple documents at once

**Features:**
- **Cost-Effective:** Only stores 32-byte hash on-chain (actual files off-chain)
- **Immutable Proof:** Blockchain timestamp proves document existence
- **Privacy:** File content stays private, only hash is public
- **Verification:** Anyone can verify document hasn't been tampered with
- **Linking:** Connect documents to escrows and invoices
- **Metadata:** Store IPFS CID or database reference
- **Document Types:** Contract, Invoice, ProofOfDelivery, LegalDocument, Specification, ComplianceCert, Other

**How It Works:**
1. **Upload:** User uploads file to database/IPFS
2. **Hash:** Generate SHA-256 hash of file
3. **Register:** Call `registerDocument()` with hash and metadata URI
4. **Link:** Optionally link to escrow or invoice
5. **Verify:** Anyone can verify authenticity by comparing hashes

**Security Model:**
- ✅ Document content remains off-chain (private)
- ✅ Hash stored on-chain (public verification)
- ✅ Timestamp proves when document was registered
- ✅ Cannot forge or backdate documents
- ✅ Minimal gas cost (~0.0001 ETH per document)

## Storage Architecture

### LibAppStorage.sol
Centralized storage using Diamond Storage pattern:

```solidity
struct AppStorage {
    // Liquidity Pool Storage
    address usdcToken;
    mapping(address => Stake) stakes;
    address[] stakers;
    uint256 totalStaked;
    uint256 totalLPs;
    uint256 minimumStake;
    
    // Governance Storage
    mapping(string => TradeFinanceRequest) requests;
    mapping(string => mapping(address => bool)) hasVoted;
    string[] requestIds;
    uint256 totalRequests;
    uint256 totalFunded;
    uint256 approvalThreshold;
    
    // Escrow Storage
    mapping(uint256 => Escrow) escrows;
    uint256 escrowCounter;
    uint256 totalEscrows;
    uint256 activeEscrows;
    
    // Invoice Storage
    mapping(uint256 => Invoice) invoices;
    mapping(string => uint256) invoiceNumberToId;
    uint256 invoiceCounter;
    uint256 totalInvoices;
    
    // Document Storage
    mapping(bytes32 => DocumentRecord) documents;
    mapping(address => bytes32[]) userDocuments;
    mapping(uint256 => bytes32[]) escrowDocuments;
    mapping(uint256 => bytes32[]) invoiceDocuments;
    uint256 totalDocuments;
}
```

### LibDiamond.sol
Core Diamond logic:
- Function selector mapping
- Facet management
- Ownership control
- Upgrade mechanism

## How It Works

### For LP Providers:
1. **Stake:** Call `stake(amount)` on Diamond address (min 100 USDC)
2. **Vote:** Call `vote(requestId, support)` to approve/reject requests
3. **Earn:** Receive proportional rewards from trade fees
4. **Unstake:** Call `unstake()` anytime to withdraw

### For Buyers/Importers:
1. **Request:** Call `createRequest()` with seller, amount, description
2. **Wait:** LPs vote on your request
3. **Approved:** ≥60% votes triggers approval
4. **Release:** Call `releaseFunds()` to send USDC to seller

### For Sellers/Exporters:
1. Buyer creates request with your address
2. Wait for LP approval
3. Receive USDC directly from Diamond contract
4. Complete trade delivery

### For Escrow (Importer/Exporter/Arbitrator):
1. **Create:** Importer calls `createEscrow()` with exporter, arbitrator, milestones, and deposits funds
2. **Sub-Wallets:** Parties add sub-wallets with role-based permissions
3. **Complete:** Exporter marks milestones as completed
4. **Release:** Importer releases payment after verifying completion
5. **Dispute:** Either party can raise dispute if needed
6. **Resolve:** Arbitrator decides fund distribution and receives fee

### For Invoicing:
1. **Create:** Payee creates invoice with `createInvoice()`
2. **View:** Payer views invoice and marks as viewed
3. **Pay:** Payer calls `payInvoice()` with ETH or tokens
4. **Verify:** On-chain payment verification and status update
5. **Track:** Check overdue invoices and payment statistics

### For Document Verification:
1. **Upload:** Upload document to database/IPFS (off-chain storage)
2. **Generate Hash:** Create SHA-256 hash of document
3. **Register:** Call `registerDocument(hash, metadataURI, docType)`
4. **Link:** Connect document to escrow/invoice with `linkDocumentToEscrow()` or `linkDocumentToInvoice()`
5. **Verify:** Anyone can call `verifyDocument(hash)` to check authenticity
6. **Batch Verify:** Use `batchVerifyDocuments()` for multiple documents
7. **Track:** Query documents by user, escrow, or invoice

## Deployment

### Networks
- **Testnet:** Base Sepolia (ChainID: 84532)
- **Mainnet:** Base (ChainID: 8453)

### Prerequisites
```bash
cd contracts
npm install
cp .env.example .env
# Add DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY and BASESCAN_API_KEY
```

### Deploy Diamond
```bash
# Testnet
npx hardhat run scripts/deploy-diamond.js --network baseSepolia

# Mainnet (when ready)
npx hardhat run scripts/deploy-diamond.js --network base
```

### Verify Contracts
```bash
npx hardhat verify --network baseSepolia <DIAMOND_ADDRESS> <OWNER> <DIAMONDCUT_ADDRESS> <USDC_ADDRESS>
```

## Deployed Contracts

**Testnet (Base Sepolia):**
```
Diamond: [TO BE DEPLOYED]
DiamondCutFacet: [TO BE DEPLOYED]
DiamondLoupeFacet: [TO BE DEPLOYED]
LiquidityPoolFacet: [TO BE DEPLOYED]
GovernanceFacet: [TO BE DEPLOYED]
EscrowFacet: [TO BE DEPLOYED]
InvoiceFacet: [TO BE DEPLOYED]
```

**Configuration:**
- USDC Token: `0x036CbD53842c5426634e7929541eC2318f3dCF7e`
- Minimum Stake: 100 USDC
- Approval Threshold: 60%

## Security Features

1. **Diamond Standard** - Battle-tested modular architecture
2. **ReentrancyGuard** - Prevents reentrancy attacks
3. **SafeERC20** - Secure token transfers (OpenZeppelin)
4. **Access Control** - Owner-only upgrade functions
5. **Vote Validation** - Only active LPs can vote
6. **Double-Vote Prevention** - One vote per LP per request

## Grant Application Benefits

### Technical Excellence
✅ **EIP-2535 Diamond Standard** - Industry-standard modular architecture  
✅ **OpenZeppelin Libraries** - Battle-tested security  
✅ **Gas Optimized** - Efficient storage and execution  
✅ **Fully Upgradeable** - Add features without redeployment  
✅ **Transparent** - All votes and decisions on-chain  

### Market Opportunity
- **Global Trade Finance Gap:** $1.5 trillion (WTO)
- **SME Financing Gap:** $5.2 trillion (IFC)
- **Blockchain Trade Finance Market:** $3.5B by 2030

### Competitive Advantages
1. **No Bank Intermediaries** - Direct LP-to-trader financing
2. **Instant Approval** - Minutes vs weeks
3. **Transparent Governance** - On-chain voting records
4. **Accessible** - Open to anyone with crypto
5. **Upgradeable** - Future-proof architecture

## Integration with Frontend

### Trade Finance Example
```typescript
// Connect to Diamond
const diamond = new ethers.Contract(
  DIAMOND_ADDRESS,
  [...LiquidityPoolFacet.abi, ...GovernanceFacet.abi],
  signer
);

// Stake USDC
await diamond.stake(ethers.parseUnits("100", 6)); // 100 USDC

// Create trade request
await diamond.createRequest(
  "REQ-001",
  sellerAddress,
  ethers.parseUnits("10000", 6),
  "Import 1000 units of product X"
);

// Vote on request
await diamond.vote("REQ-001", true); // Support

// Release funds (after approval)
await diamond.releaseFunds("REQ-001");
```

### Escrow Example
```typescript
// Connect to Diamond with EscrowFacet ABI
const diamond = new ethers.Contract(DIAMOND_ADDRESS, EscrowFacet.abi, signer);

// Create escrow with milestones
const tx = await diamond.createEscrow(
  exporterAddress,
  arbitratorAddress,
  ethers.parseUnits("100", 6), // Arbitrator fee
  Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000) + 86400 * 30, // 30 days deadline
  "Import/Export Agreement",
  "ipfs://terms-hash",
  ["Milestone 1", "Milestone 2"],
  ["Ship goods", "Deliver goods"],
  [ethers.parseUnits("5000", 6), ethers.parseUnits("5000", 6)],
  [Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000) + 86400 * 15, Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000) + 86400 * 30],
  USDC_ADDRESS,
  { value: 0 } // Or ETH amount if using ETH
);

// Complete milestone (exporter)
await diamond.completeMilestone(escrowId, 0);

// Release payment (importer)
await diamond.releaseMilestonePayment(escrowId, 0);

// Raise dispute if needed
await diamond.raiseDispute(escrowId, "Goods not delivered");

// Resolve dispute (arbitrator)
await diamond.resolveDispute(escrowId, [0, 1], [true, false]);
```

### Invoice Example
```typescript
// Connect to Diamond with InvoiceFacet ABI
const diamond = new ethers.Contract(DIAMOND_ADDRESS, InvoiceFacet.abi, signer);

// Create invoice
const invoiceTx = await diamond.createInvoice(
  "INV-2025-001",
  payerAddress,
  ethers.parseUnits("1000", 6), // 1000 USDC
  USDC_ADDRESS,
  Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000) + 86400 * 30, // 30 days due date
  "Consulting services - January 2025",
  "ipfs://invoice-terms"
);

// Payer marks as viewed
await diamond.markInvoiceViewed(invoiceId);

// Payer pays invoice
const payTx = await diamond.payInvoice(invoiceId);

// Check if overdue
const overdue = await diamond.isOverdue(invoiceId);

// Get invoice stats
const stats = await diamond.getInvoiceStats();
// Returns: { totalInvoices, paidInvoices, pendingInvoices }
```

## Roadmap

**Phase 1 (Current):** ✅ Diamond architecture implemented  
**Phase 2 (Q1 2026):** Deploy to Base Mainnet + Professional audit  
**Phase 3 (Q2 2026):** Multi-token support, credit scoring  
**Phase 4 (Q3 2026):** Enterprise partnerships, regulatory compliance

## Contact
For grant inquiries, audits, and partnerships:  
**BlockFinaX Team** - Building the future of decentralized trade finance

DEPLOYMENT_GUIDE.md.content:
# 🚀 BlockFinaX Diamond Smart Contract Deployment Guide

Complete step-by-step instructions to deploy the EIP-2535 Diamond Standard smart contracts to Base Sepolia testnet.

---

## 📋 Prerequisites

Before deploying, ensure you have:

1. **A wallet with Base Sepolia ETH** for gas fees
   - Get free testnet ETH from Base Sepolia faucet: https://www.coinbase.com/faucets/base-ethereum-goerli-faucet
   - You'll need approximately 0.01 ETH for deployment

2. **Your wallet's private key** (we'll add it securely as an environment variable)

3. **(Optional) BaseScan API Key** for contract verification
   - Get free API key: https://basescan.org/apis

---

## 🔧 Step-by-Step Deployment

### Step 1: Add Your Deployer Private Key

You need to provide your wallet's private key as an environment variable. I'll ask you for it securely:

**⚠️ SECURITY NOTE:** Your private key will be stored as a Replit secret and never exposed in code or logs.

### Step 2: Install Dependencies

```bash
cd contracts
npm install
```

### Step 3: Compile Contracts

```bash
npm run compile
```

This will:
- Compile all 6 facets (LiquidityPool, Governance, Escrow, Invoice, DiamondCut, DiamondLoupe)
- Generate ABIs and artifacts
- Verify no compilation errors

### Step 4: Deploy to Base Sepolia

```bash
npm run deploy:testnet
```

This script will:
1. ✅ Deploy DiamondCutFacet
2. ✅ Deploy Diamond proxy contract
3. ✅ Deploy DiamondLoupeFacet (introspection)
4. ✅ Deploy LiquidityPoolFacet (staking & pool management)
5. ✅ Deploy GovernanceFacet (voting system)
6. ✅ Deploy EscrowFacet (milestone payments & dispute resolution)
7. ✅ Deploy InvoiceFacet (on-chain invoice management)
8. ✅ Add all facets to Diamond via diamondCut
9. ✅ Save deployment info to `deployment-info.json`

**Expected Output:**
```
🚀 Deploying BlockFinaX Diamond (EIP-2535) Contracts...

📝 Deploying with account: 0x...
💰 Account balance: 0.05 ETH

📦 Deploying DiamondCutFacet...
✅ DiamondCutFacet: 0x...

📦 Deploying Diamond...
✅ Diamond: 0x...

[... all facets deploy ...]

✅ Deployment complete!
💾 Deployment info saved to deployment-info.json
```

### Step 5: Verify Deployment

After deployment completes, you'll have a `deployment-info.json` file with all contract addresses:

```json
{
  "network": "baseSepolia",
  "chainId": 84532,
  "diamond": "0x...",
  "facets": {
    "DiamondCutFacet": "0x...",
    "DiamondLoupeFacet": "0x...",
    "LiquidityPoolFacet": "0x...",
    "GovernanceFacet": "0x...",
    "EscrowFacet": "0x...",
    "InvoiceFacet": "0x..."
  },
  "usdcAddress": "0x036CbD53842c5426634e7929541eC2318f3dCF7e"
}
```

**Verify on BaseScan:**
- Visit: https://sepolia.basescan.org/address/[DIAMOND_ADDRESS]
- You should see the Diamond contract deployed

### Step 6: Test Contract Functions

You can test the deployed contracts using Hardhat console:

```bash
npx hardhat console --network baseSepolia
```

Then:
```javascript
const Diamond = await ethers.getContractFactory("Diamond");
const diamond = await Diamond.attach("YOUR_DIAMOND_ADDRESS");

// Test liquidity pool stats
const stats = await diamond.getPoolStats();
console.log("Pool Stats:", stats);
```

---

## 🔗 Frontend Integration

### Update Frontend Contract Address

After deployment, update the frontend to use the new Diamond contract:

1. Open `client/src/lib/staking-manager.ts`
2. Update the Diamond address:
```typescript
const DIAMOND_ADDRESS = "YOUR_DEPLOYED_DIAMOND_ADDRESS";
```

3. The frontend will automatically use all 6 facets through the Diamond proxy!

---

## 📊 What Gets Deployed

### Diamond Standard Architecture (EIP-2535)

**Main Contract:**
- **Diamond Proxy** - Single entry point, upgradeable

**6 Facets (Modules):**
1. **DiamondCutFacet** - Upgrade functionality
2. **DiamondLoupeFacet** - Contract introspection
3. **LiquidityPoolFacet** - LP staking, pool stats
4. **GovernanceFacet** - Vote on trade finance requests
5. **EscrowFacet** - Milestone payments, dispute resolution, sub-wallets
6. **InvoiceFacet** - On-chain invoice verification

**Token Integration:**
- USDC: `0x036CbD53842c5426634e7929541eC2318f3dCF7e` (Base Sepolia)

---

## 🛠️ Troubleshooting

### Issue: "Insufficient funds"
**Solution:** Get more Base Sepolia ETH from faucet

### Issue: "DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY not found"
**Solution:** Make sure you provided your private key as a secret

### Issue: "Compilation failed"
**Solution:** Run `npm run compile` again and check for errors

### Issue: "Transaction failed"
**Solution:** Check your Base Sepolia ETH balance and network connection

---

## ✅ Post-Deployment Checklist

- [ ] All 6 facets deployed successfully
- [ ] Diamond proxy contract deployed
- [ ] `deployment-info.json` created
- [ ] Contract verified on BaseScan (optional)
- [ ] Frontend updated with Diamond address
- [ ] Test staking function works
- [ ] Test escrow creation works
- [ ] Test invoice creation works

---

## 🎉 Success!

Your BlockFinaX Diamond Standard smart contracts are now live on Base Sepolia testnet!

**Next Steps:**
1. Update frontend with deployed Diamond address
2. Test all features (staking, voting, escrow, invoices)
3. Prepare for grant application submission
4. (Optional) Deploy to Base mainnet when ready

**Deployed Features:**
- ✅ Decentralized liquidity pools
- ✅ Governance voting system
- ✅ Smart contract escrow with milestones
- ✅ On-chain invoice verification
- ✅ Multi-party trade finance
- ✅ Dispute resolution
- ✅ Upgradeable architecture

---

## 📞 Support

If you encounter issues:
1. Check the Hardhat console output for errors
2. Verify your Base Sepolia ETH balance
3. Review the deployment script logs
4. Check BaseScan for transaction status

# 🔍 Manual Basescan Verification Guide

All your contracts have been **flattened** (combined into single files) for easy manual verification on Basescan.

## 📁 Flattened Contract Files

All files are located in: `contracts/flattened/`

1. **Diamond-flattened.sol** (14 KB)
2. **DiamondCutFacet-flattened.sol** (7.9 KB)
3. **DiamondLoupeFacet-flattened.sol** (9.5 KB)
4. **LiquidityPoolFacet-flattened.sol** (35 KB)
5. **GovernanceFacet-flattened.sol** (29 KB)
6. **EscrowFacet-flattened.sol** (37 KB)
7. **InvoiceFacet-flattened.sol** (30 KB)
8. **DocumentFacet-flattened.sol** (13 KB)

---

## 🚀 How to Verify Each Contract

### Step 1: Download the Flattened File

1. In Replit's file explorer (left sidebar)
2. Navigate to `contracts` → `flattened`
3. Right-click on the file (e.g., `Diamond-flattened.sol`)
4. Select "Download"

### Step 2: Go to Basescan Verification Page

For each contract, go to:
```
https://sepolia.basescan.org/verifyContract?a=CONTRACT_ADDRESS
```

Replace `CONTRACT_ADDRESS` with the actual address.

### Step 3: Fill Out the Form

**Compiler Type:** `Solidity (Single file)`

**Compiler Version:** `v0.8.20+commit.a1b79de6`

**Open Source License:** `MIT License (MIT)` or `No License (None)`

**Optimization Enabled:** `Yes`

**Runs (Optimizer):** `200`

**Enter the Solidity Contract Code:** 
- Open the downloaded flattened file
- Copy ALL the code
- Paste it into the text box

### Step 4: Constructor Arguments (Only for Diamond)

**For Diamond Contract ONLY**, you need constructor arguments:

**Contract Address:** `0x8dD38f78a34b12f967c635252FB03b2f055988f6`

**Constructor Arguments (ABI-encoded):**
```
0x000000000000000000000000ef5bed7c221c85a2c88e3c0223ee45482d6f037d000000000000000000000000ac939c0897981abc0711ec4e37527f13106180fc000000000000000000000000036cbd53842c5426634e7929541ec2318f3dcf7e
```

**For All Facets:** Leave constructor arguments EMPTY (they have none)

---

## 📋 Contract Addresses to Verify

### Main Contract
- **Diamond:** `0x8dD38f78a34b12f967c635252FB03b2f055988f6`

### Facets (No Constructor Arguments)
1. **DiamondCutFacet:** `0xaC939C0897981Abc0711ec4e37527F13106180fc`
2. **DiamondLoupeFacet:** `0x94e1436180C4c577D98e769D1830F39f62F0b122`
3. **LiquidityPoolFacet:** `0x090a3cC9A71e2272f6a681C2bA940C71D36c239f`
4. **GovernanceFacet:** `0x956410d5be6ad138e97f51b40C1E53e3808ee628`
5. **EscrowFacet:** `0xAF57C4C47AA4afB41f44104075aFC177e5AE9885`
6. **InvoiceFacet:** `0xE94B3880C7846E162DAAaEFD83d972f5B19cf162`
7. **DocumentFacet:** `0xFF632a8112C41465E4a39B2406f67871A2eFECde`

---

## 💡 Pro Tips

1. **Start with the Facets** (easier, no constructor arguments)
2. **Save Diamond for last** (has constructor arguments)
3. **Double-check compiler settings** match exactly
4. **Copy the ENTIRE flattened file** (Ctrl+A, Ctrl+C)
5. **Wait a few seconds** after submitting for verification to process

---

## ✅ Success!

Once verified, your contracts will have:
- ✓ Green checkmark on Basescan

- ✓ Read/Write functionality directly on Basescan

# ⚙️ EXACT Basescan Verification Settings

## 🚨 CRITICAL: Your contracts use viaIR compilation!

This is why you're getting the "Unable to find matching bytecode" error. You MUST include the viaIR setting.

---

## 📋 EXACT Settings for All Contracts

### Basic Settings:

1. **Compiler Type:** `Solidity (Single file)`

2. **Compiler Version:** `v0.8.20+commit.a1b79de6`

3. **Open Source License Type:** `MIT License (MIT)` or `3) No License (None)`

4. **Optimization Enabled:** `Yes`

5. **Runs (Optimizer):** `200`

6. **EVM Version:** `paris` *(or leave as default)*

### 🔥 MOST IMPORTANT - Advanced Settings:

**Scroll down** to find "Misc Settings" or "Constructor Arguments ABI-encoded" section

Look for these options:

- **☑️ Enable "Via IR"** (if available)
- **OR** Look for **"viaIR"** checkbox and check it
- **OR** Look for **"Compiler Settings"** and add: `{"viaIR": true}`

---

## 🎯 If You Can't Find viaIR Option

If Basescan doesn't show a viaIR checkbox, try this:

### Method 1: JSON Input Method

Some Basescan versions allow JSON compiler settings:

Look for **"Enter the Solidity Contract Code"** page and find:
- "Or use Compiler Settings JSON (optional)"
- Paste this:

```json
{
  "optimizer": {
    "enabled": true,
    "runs": 200
  },
  "viaIR": true
}
```

### Method 2: Use Standard Verification API

I can help you try API-based verification instead if the manual form doesn't work.

---

## 📝 Complete Checklist for DocumentFacet

Let's verify **DocumentFacet** first (smallest, easiest):

**Address:** `0xFF632a8112C41465E4a39B2406f67871A2eFECde`

**URL:** https://sepolia.basescan.org/verifyContract?a=0xFF632a8112C41465E4a39B2406f67871A2eFECde

### Settings:
- [ ] Compiler Type: `Solidity (Single file)`
- [ ] Compiler Version: `v0.8.20+commit.a1b79de6`
- [ ] License: `MIT` or `None`
- [ ] Optimization: `Yes`
- [ ] Runs: `200`
- [ ] **viaIR: Enabled** ✅ (CRITICAL!)
- [ ] Paste code from: `contracts/flattened/DocumentFacet-flattened.sol`
- [ ] Constructor Arguments: *(leave empty)*

---

## 🆘 Still Not Working?

If the bytecode still doesn't match, we have 2 options:

**Option A:** I can recompile WITHOUT viaIR (standard compilation) and redeploy
- ⚠️ This means new contract addresses

**Option B:** Use Hardhat's API verification (I'll fix the API key issue)
- ✅ Keeps existing contracts

---

Let me know:
1. **Can you find a viaIR checkbox** on the Basescan form?
2. **Or do you see "Compiler Settings JSON"** option?

I'll help you get this working! 🚀

docs/POOL_GUARANTEE_IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMA.content:
# Pool Guarantee System - Implementation Summary

## ✅ Database Schema Implemented

The following tables have been added to support Pool Guarantee with **goods as collateral**:

### 1. **deliveryProofs** (Proof of Shipment/Delivery)
Tracks all shipping documentation and proof of delivery:
- Bill of Lading (hash & number)
- Tracking numbers & logistics provider
- Delivery confirmation (date, address, received by)
- Supporting documents (inspection, photos, customs, quality certificates)
- Verification status (pending, verified, disputed, rejected)
- Buyer dispute handling

### 2. **goodsCollateral** (Detailed Goods Tracking)
Comprehensive tracking of goods used as collateral:
- **Goods description**: Type, quantity, unit price, total value
- **Valuation**: Market price, over-collateralization ratio (default 110%)
- **Inspection**: Inspector address, quality grade, status
- **Insurance**: Provider, policy number, coverage amount, documents
- **Storage & Logistics**: Warehouse location, storage provider, shipment dates
- **Bill of Lading (BoL)**: Tokenized BoL, NFT contract address, issuance tracking
- **Liquidation**: Pre-arranged buyers, liquidation partners, estimated value

### 3. **guaranteeClaims** (Seller Claims When Buyer Defaults)
Manages seller claims when buyer fails to pay:
- Claim amount & reason
- Links to delivery proof evidence
- Supporting documents
- Review & approval workflow
- Payment tracking (transaction hash, amount, date)
- Buyer dispute resolution

### 4. **guaranteeIssuanceFees** (Fee Tracking & Distribution)
Tracks issuance fees paid by buyers:
- Fee percentage & amount (e.g., 1.5% of $10M = $150K)
- Payment status & transaction hash
- Distribution to stakers (70-80%) and treasury (20-30%)
- Distribution status tracking

---

## 🔄 Integration with Existing Tables

The Pool Guarantee system **extends** existing trade finance infrastructure:

### **Leverages `tradeFinanceRequests` Table**
- Already has buyer/seller addresses
- Voting mechanism (votesFor, votesAgainst)
- Status tracking (pending, approved, active, completed)
- Collateral fields (description, value)
- Performance bond support

### **Uses `liquidityPoolStakes` Table**
- Existing staker records
- Voting power calculation
- Rewards tracking

### **Uses `tradeFinanceVotes` Table**
- Staker voting on guarantee approvals
- Vote tallying and approval threshold (60%)

---

## 📊 Complete Pool Guarantee Workflow (Database Level)

```
1. BUYER APPLICATION
   └─> Insert into `tradeFinanceRequests`
       - buyerAddress, sellerAddress
       - requestedAmount (e.g., $10M)
       - collateralDescription, collateralValue
       - tradeDescription
       - status: "pending"
   
   └─> Insert into `goodsCollateral`
       - goodsType: "sugar"
       - quantity: 10000 tons
       - totalValue: $11M (110% over-collateralized)
       - inspectionRequired: true
       - insuranceRequired: true
       - status: "pending"
   
   └─> Insert into `guaranteeIssuanceFees`
       - feePercentage: 1.5%
       - feeAmount: $150K
       - payerAddress: buyer
       - paymentStatus: "pending"

2. STAKER VOTING
   └─> Stakers vote via `tradeFinanceVotes`
   └─> If 60%+ approve → status: "approved"

3. GOODS VERIFICATION
   └─> Update `goodsCollateral`:
       - inspectionStatus: "passed"
       - qualityGrade: "A"
       - insuranceDocumentHash: "ipfs://..."
       - status: "verified"

4. SHIPMENT & DELIVERY
   └─> Seller ships goods
   └─> Insert into `deliveryProofs`:
       - billOfLadingHash, trackingNumber
       - logisticsProvider: "Maersk"
       - verificationStatus: "pending"
   
   └─> Update `goodsCollateral`:
       - billOfLadingIssued: true
       - billOfLadingNumber: "MAEU123456"
       - actualShipmentDate: timestamp
       - status: "in_transit"

5A. BUYER PAYS (Happy Path)
   └─> Buyer pays seller directly
   └─> Update `tradeFinanceRequests`:
       - status: "completed"
       - completedAt: timestamp
   
   └─> Update `deliveryProofs`:
       - verificationStatus: "verified"
       - receivedBy: buyer signature
   
   └─> Update `guaranteeIssuanceFees`:
       - distributionStatus: "distributed"
       - stakersShare: $120K (80%)
       - treasuryShare: $30K (20%)

5B. BUYER DEFAULTS (Sad Path)
   └─> Payment deadline passes
   
   └─> Insert into `guaranteeClaims`:
       - claimantAddress: seller
       - claimAmount: $10M
       - claimReason: "buyer_default"
       - deliveryProofId: [link to proof]
       - status: "pending"
   
   └─> Governance reviews claim
   
   └─> Update `guaranteeClaims`:
       - status: "approved"
       - approvedAmount: $10M
       - reviewedBy: governance
   
   └─> Pool pays seller from staker funds
   
   └─> Update `guaranteeClaims`:
       - paymentTransactionHash: "0x..."
       - paidAmount: $10M
       - paidAt: timestamp
       - status: "paid"
   
   └─> Update `goodsCollateral`:
       - status: "liquidated"
   
   └─> Pool sells goods to recover funds
       - Contact liquidationPartner
       - Sell for ~$9.5M (estimated)
       - Net loss: $500K (covered by fees + staker pool)
```

---

## 🎯 Key Design Decisions

### **Goods as Primary Collateral**
- Goods must be worth **110-120%** of guarantee amount
- Small cash collateral (5-10%) as buffer for liquidation costs
- Bill of Lading tracked on-chain (hash) + optional NFT tokenization

### **Fee Structure (Recommended)**
- **Issuance fee:** 1-2% of transaction value
- **Distribution:** 70-80% to stakers, 20-30% to treasury
- **No separate success fee** (issuance fee covers service)

### **Verification Requirements**
- Independent inspection required (inspectorAddress)
- Cargo insurance mandatory (insuranceProvider, policyNumber)
- Bill of Lading required for shipment
- Delivery proof with multiple documents (tracking, photos, certificates)

### **Liquidation Strategy**
- Pre-arranged buyers (preLiquidationBuyers JSON array)
- Liquidation partner (broker/commodity trader)
- Estimated liquidation value tracked
- Quick sale target: 90-95% of market value

---

## 🚀 Next Steps

### ✅ Completed
1. Database schema design
2. Pool Guarantee tables created (4 new tables)
3. Integration with existing trade finance tables
4. Schema pushed to PostgreSQL database

### 🔄 In Progress
2. Create PoolGuaranteeFacet smart contract

### 📋 Pending
3. Deploy PoolGuaranteeFacet to Base Sepolia
4. Build backend API routes
5. Create frontend UI
6. Test end-to-end workflow

---

## 📄 Related Documentation
- **Planning Document:** `docs/POOL_GUARANTEE_SYSTEM.md` (full concept & questions)
- **Database Schema:** `shared/schema.ts` (lines 728-914)
- **Smart Contracts:** `contracts/src/facets/` (to be created)

---

**Last Updated:** October 23, 2025  
**Status:** Database schema implemented ✅

# IRREVOCABLE POOL GUARANTEE

**GOVERNING RULES:** This guarantee is subject to the Uniform Rules for Demand Guarantees (URDG 758, ICC Publication No. 758, 2010 Revision) of the International Chamber of Commerce.

**FORM OF GUARANTEE:** IRREVOCABLE

---

## PARTIES

**APPLICANT (Principal)**  
The Rekki Group Ltd

Accra, Ghana

BlockFinaX Treasury Pool  
Pool Address: 0x9876543210fedcba9876543210fedcba98765432  
Authorized Capital: USD 1,500,000.00 (USDC equivalent)  
Status: Fully Funded and Operational

---

## GUARANTEE AMOUNT

**Currency Code:** USD (US DOLLAR)  
**Maximum Amount:** USD 50,000.00 (FIFTY THOUSAND UNITED STATES DOLLARS) in USDC stablecoin

**Percentage Credit Amount Tolerance:** 0/0 (ZERO TOLERANCE)

**Collateral Security:** USD 5,000.00 (FIVE THOUSAND UNITED STATES DOLLARS) deposited by Applicant in escrow, representing 10% of guaranteed amount

---

## UNDERLYING TRANSACTION

**Sales and Purchase Agreement Reference:** ST1019WDF4EG54U.CIF.MT700.1T5WFNE dated 22 August 2025

**Description of Goods:**  
Industrial Machinery Parts - CNC Components conforming to ISO 9001 quality standards

**Item Name:** CNC Precision Components  
**Quantity:** 500 units  
**Unit Price:** USD 100.00 per unit  
**Total Contract Value:** USD 50,000.00  
**Country of Origin:** Singapore

**Delivery Terms:** FOB (Free on Board) Singapore (INCOTERMS 2020)

**Shipping Details:**  
- Transport Document: Bill of Lading No. BOL-2025-SG-NY-4567  
- Port of Loading: Singapore  
- Port of Discharge: New York, United States  
- Latest Shipment Date: 10 November 2025  
- Latest Delivery Date: 15 November 2025  
- Partial Shipments: NOT ALLOWED  
- Transhipment: NOT ALLOWED

---

## PAYMENT STRUCTURE

This guarantee operates under a milestone-based payment mechanism with funds held in escrow.

| Milestone | Description | Amount (USD) | Due Date |
|-----------|-------------|--------------|----------|
| I | Manufacturing Completion Certificate | 15,000.00 | 5 November 2025 |
| II | Shipment and Bill of Lading Presentation | 20,000.00 | 10 November 2025 |
| III | Delivery Confirmation and Final Inspection | 15,000.00 | 15 November 2025 |

**Total Guaranteed Amount:** USD 50,000.00

**Drawing Exceeding This Guarantee Amount:** NOT ACCEPTABLE

---

## ARTICLE 1: GUARANTEE UNDERTAKING

We, BlockFinaX Treasury Pool, hereby irrevocably and unconditionally undertake to pay to the Beneficiary any sum or sums not exceeding in total the amount stated above upon receipt of the Beneficiary's complying demand supported by the documents specified in Article 2 herein.

This guarantee is payable in USDC cryptocurrency and shall be transferred directly to the Beneficiary's wallet address specified above.

---

## ARTICLE 2: DOCUMENTS REQUIRED FOR PAYMENT

The Beneficiary's demand for payment under this guarantee shall be supported by the following documents:

**2.1 For Milestone I Payment (USD 15,000.00):**

1. **Commercial Invoice** in one (1) original and three (3) copies, certifying that the quality, quantity and description of goods are strictly in accordance with Sales and Purchase Agreement Ref No. ST1019WDF4EG54U.CIF.MT700.1T5WFNE dated 22 August 2025, signed and sealed by Beneficiary.

2. **Manufacturing Completion Certificate** in one (1) original and three (3) copies, issued and signed by Beneficiary's authorized representative.

3. **Quality Inspection Report** from manufacturer in one (1) original and three (3) copies.

4. **Beneficiary's Signed Statement** confirming milestone completion, uploaded to platform with cryptographic signature.

**2.2 For Milestone II Payment (USD 20,000.00):**

1. **Full Set (3/3) Original Signed Clean "On Board" Ocean Bill of Lading** consigned to order of Guarantor, notify the Applicant, marked "Freight Prepaid" or "Freight to Collect" as agreed.

2. **Certificate of Origin** in one (1) original and three (3) copies issued by Chamber of Commerce of Singapore.

3. **Commercial Invoice** in one (1) original and three (3) copies as per 2.1 above.

4. **Packing List** verified by Beneficiary, certifying that the quality, quantity and description of goods shipped are strictly in accordance with Sales and Purchase Agreement Ref No. ST1019WDF4EG54U.CIF.MT700.1T5WFNE dated 22 August 2025, in one (1) original and three (3) copies.

5. **Marine Insurance Policy or Certificate** covering at least 110 percent of the invoice value against Institute Cargo Clauses (All Risks), War, S.R.C.C., Theft, Pilferage and Non-Delivery, Short Shipment with claims payable at destination. Settling agent's name and address must be indicated.

6. **Proof of Shipment Departure** from Port of Loading (Singapore).

**2.3 For Milestone III Payment (USD 15,000.00):**

1. **Delivery Receipt** signed by Applicant or appointed agent at Port of Discharge.

2. **Goods Received Note (GRN)** from customs clearance.

3. **Inspection Certificate** in one (1) original and three (3) copies issued, signed and sealed by independent inspector (SGS, Bureau Veritas, Intertek, or TÜV Rheinland), confirming conformity to specifications.

4. **Beneficiary's Final Statement** confirming goods delivery and acceptance, with cryptographic signature.

**2.4 General Document Requirements:**

- All documents must be presented within seven (7) calendar days of the relevant milestone due date
- All documents bearing a date prior to the issuing of this guarantee are not acceptable
- All documents must bear this guarantee number and date
- All documents must be dated, sealed and manually or digitally signed by Beneficiary
- All dates on all documents must follow SWIFT format (YYMMDD) or ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD)
- Documents may be presented in digital format with cryptographic verification
- All documents must be originally issued in English language
- Partial claims are permitted for individual milestones
- Scanned copies are acceptable if originals are deposited with recognized document custodian

---

## ARTICLE 3: PAYMENT MECHANISM

**3.1 Automatic Release Provision**

If the Applicant fails to confirm milestone completion within seven (7) calendar days of the due date specified in the payment structure table, funds for that milestone shall be automatically released to the Beneficiary without further documentation.

**3.2 Disbursement Process**

Upon presentation of complying documents or expiry of the seven-day confirmation period, payment shall be automatically transferred to the Beneficiary's wallet address within twenty-four (24) hours.

**3.3 Confirmation of Payment**

Payment confirmation shall be evidenced by blockchain transaction record and shall be deemed final and irrevocable upon execution.

**3.4 Payment Date**

The Guarantor will effect payment on the due date under this guarantee to the Beneficiary's account, in accordance with their instruction, upon receipt of the required documents which must comply fully with the guarantee terms.

---

## ARTICLE 4: PERIOD FOR PRESENTATION

**4.1 Presentation Period**

Documents must be presented within twenty-one (21) calendar days from shipment date and within the validity of this guarantee.

**4.2 Latest Date for Presentation**

All documents must be presented on or before 15 December 2025 (expiry date).

---

## ARTICLE 5: TRANSPORT DOCUMENTS AND BILL OF LADING

**5.1 Bill of Lading Requirements**

The Bill of Lading must:
- Be a full set (3/3) of original signed clean "on board" ocean bills of lading
- Be issued or endorsed to the order of the Guarantor
- Notify the Applicant
- Show the goods as shipped on board a named vessel
- Indicate the Port of Loading as Singapore
- Indicate the Port of Discharge as New York, United States
- Be dated on or before 10 November 2025
- Be marked "Freight Prepaid" or "Freight to Collect" as agreed between parties
- Not be charter party B/L, stale B/L, short form blank back B/L, or non-negotiable seaway bills of lading

**5.2 Original Documents**

- The Beneficiary must present the original Bill of Lading (3/3 originals)
- We may require duplicate original documents before processing payment
- The original Bill of Lading must be surrendered to the carrier for goods release

**5.3 Document Custody**

Until final payment of Milestone III:
- The original Bill of Lading shall be held by the Guarantor or appointed document custodian
- The Guarantor shall release the original Bill of Lading to the Applicant only upon:
  - Confirmation of goods receipt at Port of Discharge, AND
  - Completion of customs clearance, AND
  - Payment of all amounts due to Beneficiary

**5.4 Electronic Bill of Lading**

If an electronic Bill of Lading (e-BL) is used:
- It must comply with UNCITRAL Model Law on Electronic Transferable Records
- The e-BL platform must provide legally equivalent control and transferability
- Both parties must consent to electronic format prior to shipment

---

## ARTICLE 6: GOODS INSPECTION RIGHTS

**6.1 Pre-Shipment Inspection**

The Applicant has the right to:
- Conduct pre-shipment inspection at the Beneficiary's manufacturing facility or warehouse
- Appoint a third-party inspection company (costs borne by Applicant)
- Verify quantity, quality, and specifications against the contract
- Request corrections if goods do not conform to specifications

**6.2 Post-Delivery Inspection**

Upon delivery of goods to the Port of Discharge or final destination:
- The Applicant shall have ten (10) business days from customs clearance to conduct a full inspection
- Inspection must verify:
  - Quantity matches Bill of Lading and commercial invoice
  - Quality conforms to ISO 9001 standards and contract specifications
  - No damage occurred during transit (subject to insurance claims)
  - Packaging is adequate and unopened

**6.3 Notice of Non-Conformity**

If the Applicant discovers defects or non-conformity:
- Written notice must be given to the Beneficiary within five (5) business days of discovery
- Notice must specify the nature and extent of defects with photographic or video evidence
- Minor defects (less than 5% of shipment value) may be subject to price adjustment
- Major defects may result in goods rejection and payment suspension

**6.4 Third-Party Independent Inspection**

Both parties may agree to appoint an independent inspection company such as:
- SGS (Société Générale de Surveillance)
- Bureau Veritas
- Intertek
- TÜV Rheinland

The inspection company's report shall be binding on both parties. Costs shall be borne equally unless defects are found, in which case the Beneficiary bears all costs.

**6.5 Hidden Defects**

Defects not discoverable through reasonable inspection:
- Must be notified within thirty (30) days of discovery
- Beneficiary remains liable for latent defects within warranty period (if applicable)
- Claims for hidden defects do not affect payments already made

---

## ARTICLE 7: TITLE, RISK, AND OWNERSHIP

**7.1 Transfer of Title**

Under FOB (Free on Board) shipping terms per INCOTERMS 2020:
- Title to the goods passes from Beneficiary to Applicant when goods cross the ship's rail at the Port of Loading (Singapore)
- The Beneficiary's obligation is complete upon delivery to the carrier

**7.2 Risk of Loss**

- Risk of loss or damage transfers to the Applicant upon loading at Port of Loading
- The Beneficiary is not responsible for loss or damage during transit
- The Applicant must obtain marine insurance to cover transit risks

**7.3 Retention of Title**

Notwithstanding transfer of risk:
- The Beneficiary retains legal title to the goods until full payment of all milestones
- The Applicant obtains possession but not ownership during the inspection period
- Upon final payment of Milestone III, full legal title transfers to the Applicant
- If payment is not completed, the Beneficiary may reclaim the goods

**7.4 Insurance Requirements**

- The Applicant must obtain "All Risks" marine cargo insurance covering 110% of the shipment value
- Insurance must cover Institute Cargo Clauses (All Risks), War, S.R.C.C., Theft, Pilferage and Non-Delivery, Short Shipment
- Insurance must be valid from Port of Loading to final destination
- Insurance certificate must name both Beneficiary and Applicant as insured parties
- Loss payee shall be the Guarantor until final payment is completed
- Claims must be payable at destination
- Settling agent's name and address must be indicated

---

## ARTICLE 8: WAREHOUSE CUSTODY AND GOODS RELEASE

**8.1 Customs Clearance and Storage**

Upon arrival at Port of Discharge:
- Goods shall be cleared through customs by the Applicant or appointed customs broker
- If goods require warehouse storage pending inspection or payment, they shall be stored in a bonded warehouse approved by both parties
- Storage costs (demurrage, warehousing fees) shall be borne by the Applicant

**8.2 Warehouse Custodian**

If a third-party warehouse is used:
- The warehouse operator must be licensed and insured
- A Warehouse Receipt shall be issued to the Guarantor
- Goods may only be released upon written instruction from the Guarantor
- The warehouse operator must maintain adequate security and insurance coverage

**8.3 Release of Goods**

Goods shall be released to the Applicant only when all of the following conditions are met:
- Original Bill of Lading (full set 3/3) surrendered to the carrier or warehouse custodian
- All customs duties and taxes paid
- Goods inspection completed without material defects
- Final payment (Milestone III) confirmed or released to Beneficiary
- Written release instruction issued by Guarantor

**8.4 Demurrage and Detention**

- If goods remain in port or warehouse beyond the free period:
  - Demurrage charges (port storage fees) shall be borne by the Applicant
  - Detention charges (container usage fees) shall be borne by the Applicant
- The Applicant must arrange timely collection to avoid escalating charges
- The Beneficiary is not liable for storage costs after goods are delivered to the carrier

**8.5 Collateral Manager (if applicable)**

For high-value shipments, a Collateral Management Agreement may be established:
- An independent collateral manager takes legal custody of goods
- Issues warehouse receipt to the Guarantor
- Provides regular inventory reports
- Releases goods only upon Guarantor's written authorization
- Costs borne by the Applicant

---

## ARTICLE 9: EXPIRY AND CLAIMS

**9.1 Expiry Date**

This guarantee shall expire on 15 December 2025 at the place of expiry (New York, United States). Any claim under this guarantee must be received by the Guarantor on or before the expiry date.

**9.2 Reduction and Release**

This guarantee shall be automatically reduced by each milestone payment made. Upon final payment of all milestones or expiry date (whichever occurs first), this guarantee shall be automatically terminated and the Applicant's collateral shall be released.

**9.3 Extension**

This guarantee may be extended by mutual written agreement of all parties executed through cryptographic signatures. Extension requests must be submitted at least ten (10) business days before expiry.

---

## ARTICLE 10: APPLICABLE RULES

**10.1 Governing Rules**

This guarantee is subject to the Uniform Rules for Demand Guarantees (URDG 758), ICC Publication No. 758, except to the extent that the terms of this guarantee expressly provide otherwise.

**10.2 Execution Method**

Payments under this guarantee are executed through automated smart contract technology on blockchain infrastructure, ensuring immediate and transparent settlement.

**10.3 Verification Standards**

Document authenticity shall be verified through:
- Cryptographic verification mechanisms integrated with the platform
- Facial examination of documents for compliance with guarantee terms
- Document hash verification on blockchain

**10.4 INCOTERMS**

The shipping terms FOB (Free on Board) are as defined in INCOTERMS 2020 published by the International Chamber of Commerce.

---

## ARTICLE 11: EXAMINATION OF DOCUMENTS

**11.1 Documentary Compliance**

The Guarantor deals in documents only and is in no way concerned with the underlying sales contract or the goods to which the documents may relate.

**11.2 Examination Standard**

All documents presented must be in strict compliance with the terms of this guarantee. The Guarantor shall examine documents for facial compliance only.

**11.3 Examination Period**

The Guarantor has five (5) business days from receipt of documents to examine and determine compliance per URDG 758 Article 20.

**11.4 Discrepant Documents**

- If documents are discrepant, the Guarantor will notify the Beneficiary within five (5) business days listing all discrepancies
- Discrepant documents will be subject to a discrepancy handling fee of USD 200 which will be for Beneficiary's account
- The Guarantor will seek the Applicant's approval to take up documents despite discrepancies
- If the Applicant accepts any such discrepant presentations, this will not imply that the guarantee is thereby amended or extended

---

## ARTICLE 12: DISPUTE RESOLUTION

**12.1 Appointed Arbitrator**

Disputes arising under this guarantee shall be submitted to:  
Arbitrator Address: 0x5555aaaa6666bbbb7777cccc8888dddd9999eeee

**12.2 Arbitration Process**

- Either party may raise a dispute through the platform interface
- The Arbitrator shall review submitted evidence and issue a binding decision within fourteen (14) calendar days
- The Arbitrator's decision shall be executed automatically
- The Arbitrator's decision is final and binding on all parties

**12.3 Dispute Categories**

Arbitration may be initiated for:
- Non-conforming goods or quality disputes
- Document discrepancies
- Delayed shipment or delivery
- Payment disputes
- Insurance claims

**12.4 Costs**

The party against whom the decision is made shall bear all arbitration costs.

---

## ARTICLE 13: COLLATERAL SECURITY

**13.1 Applicant's Collateral**

The Applicant has deposited USD 5,000.00 in USDC as collateral security, representing ten percent (10%) of the guaranteed amount. This collateral is held in escrow.

**13.2 Collateral Release**

Upon successful completion of all milestones and full payment to the Beneficiary, the collateral shall be automatically returned to the Applicant's wallet address.

**13.3 Collateral Forfeiture**

In the event of Applicant's breach of contract or failure to perform obligations, the collateral may be transferred to the Beneficiary as determined by the Arbitrator or as provided in the underlying sales contract.

---

## ARTICLE 14: CHARGES

**14.1 Fee Structure**

All charges other than issuing charges are for the account of the Beneficiary, including but not limited to:
- Discrepancy handling fees (USD 200 per occurrence)
- Document courier fees
- Third-party inspection fees (unless defects found)
- Platform transaction fees

**14.2 Blockchain Transaction Costs**

The Applicant shall bear all blockchain transaction costs (gas fees) associated with guarantee operations, including:
- Guarantee issuance
- Milestone payments
- Collateral deposit and release

---

## ARTICLE 15: BENEFICIARY'S RIGHTS

The Beneficiary under this guarantee has the following irrevocable rights:

**15.1** To claim payment for each milestone upon presentation of complying documents

**15.2** To receive automatic payment if Applicant fails to confirm milestone completion within the seven (7) day period

**15.3** To access real-time guarantee status and payment records

**15.4** To raise disputes in accordance with Article 12 above

**15.5** To verify all transactions and documentation through the platform

**15.6** To retain legal title to goods until full payment is received (per Article 7.3)

**15.7** To appoint carriers and arrange shipment under FOB terms

**15.8** To present documents in digital format with cryptographic verification

---

## ARTICLE 16: APPLICANT'S OBLIGATIONS

The Applicant under this guarantee agrees to:

**16.1** Maintain sufficient USDC balance to honor all milestone payments

**16.2** Confirm milestone completions within seven (7) calendar days of the due date or provide valid reasons for non-confirmation

**16.3** Accept goods conforming to contract specifications and quality standards

**16.4** Not unreasonably withhold confirmation of milestone completion

**16.5** Bear all blockchain transaction costs associated with guarantee operations

**16.6** Obtain marine cargo insurance covering transit risks per Article 7.4

**16.7** Arrange customs clearance and pay all import duties and taxes

**16.8** Conduct goods inspection within ten (10) business days of delivery

**16.9** Bear all warehousing, demurrage, and storage costs

**16.10** Surrender original Bill of Lading (full set 3/3) for goods release

**16.11** Pay all charges as specified in Article 14

---

## ARTICLE 17: GUARANTEE CHARACTERISTICS

**17.1 Irrevocable Nature**

This guarantee is irrevocable and cannot be amended or cancelled without the express written consent of all parties, evidenced by signed transactions from all parties' wallet addresses.

**17.2 Independence**

This guarantee is independent of the underlying sales contract and the Guarantor's obligation is in no way contingent upon the Guarantor's receipt of payment from the Applicant.

**17.3 Documentary Nature**

The Guarantor deals in documents only and is in no way concerned with the underlying sales contract or the goods to which the documents may relate. The Guarantor's obligation is to examine documents for facial compliance with the terms of this guarantee.

**17.4 Transferability**

This guarantee is transferable with the Guarantor's prior written consent, evidenced by signed on-chain transactions from all parties.

**17.5 Operative Instrument**

This is an operative instrument and no separate confirmation shall follow.

---

## ARTICLE 18: SANCTIONS AND COMPLIANCE

**18.1 Sanctions Compliance**

This is to confirm that this underlying transaction is not directly or indirectly related to:
- Iran, Syria, Cuba, North Korea, Russia, Crimea
- Any OFAC sanctioned entity or individual
- Any entity or individual subject to sanctions/embargoes imposed by regulatory authorities/agencies (including USA, UK, EU, UN)

**18.2 Regulatory Compliance**

All parties to this guarantee are advised that specific sanctions/embargoes are imposed by regulatory authorities against certain countries, entities, individuals, vessels, and goods. Under these measures, the Guarantor may not be able to engage in transactions (accept or process any documents or settle any transactions) that in its opinion would breach applicable sanctions.

**18.3 Anti-Money Laundering (AML)**

All parties confirm compliance with applicable Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorism Financing regulations.

---

## ARTICLE 19: LIABILITY AND DISCLAIMERS

**19.1 Guarantor's Liability**

The Guarantor assumes no liability or responsibility whatsoever and howsoever arising for:
- Any damages or consequences arising out of delay, loss in transit, or mutilation of documents
- Errors in transmission or translation of documents or messages
- The form, sufficiency, accuracy, genuineness, or legal effect of any document
- The general or particular conditions stipulated in documents or superimposed thereon
- The description, quantity, weight, quality, condition, packing, delivery, value, or existence of goods represented by documents
- The good faith, acts, omissions, solvency, performance, or standing of the consignor, carrier, forwarder, consignee, or insurer

**19.2 Force Majeure**

The Guarantor shall not be liable for delay or non-performance caused by circumstances beyond its reasonable control, including but not limited to:
- Network congestion or technical failures
- Acts of God (typhoons, earthquakes, floods)
- War, civil unrest, or terrorism
- Government actions or trade embargoes
- Port closures or labor strikes
- Pandemic or epidemic events

Neither party shall be liable for delays in shipment, delivery, or inspection caused by force majeure events. The affected party must notify the other party within five (5) business days.

---

## ARTICLE 20: VERIFICATION AND AUTHENTICATION

**20.1 Transaction Record**

This guarantee has been recorded on the Base blockchain network with a permanent, immutable record of issuance and all subsequent actions.

**20.2 Verification Method**

Any party may verify the authenticity and current status of this guarantee through:
- The BlockFinaX platform dashboard
- Public blockchain explorers
- Direct smart contract queries at Pool Address: 0x9876543210fedcba9876543210fedcba98765432

**20.3 Transparency**

All transactions, payments, and actions related to this guarantee are permanently recorded and cannot be altered or deleted, providing complete transparency and audit trail.

**20.4 Document Hash Verification**

All uploaded documents are hashed and recorded on-chain for verification and non-repudiation.

---

## ARTICLE 21: NOTICES AND COMMUNICATIONS

**21.1 Electronic Communications**

All communications related to this guarantee shall be made through:
- The platform interface with cryptographic signatures
- Signed messages to parties' respective wallet addresses
- Email to registered addresses (for supplementary communication)

**21.2 Deemed Receipt**

A communication shall be deemed received when confirmed on the blockchain network or upon email delivery receipt.

**21.3 Notices for Disputes**

Formal dispute notices must include:
- Clear description of the issue
- Supporting documentation or evidence
- Requested remedy
- Reference to relevant guarantee article

**21.4 Notification Requirements**

Upon negotiation or payment, the platform must record on-chain:
- The guarantee number
- Payment amount
- Date of shipment and negotiation/payment
- Confirmation that all terms and conditions have been complied with

---

## ADDITIONAL CONDITIONS

**A1.** Documents bearing a date prior to the issuing of this guarantee (24 October 2025) are not acceptable.

**A2.** All shipping documents must bear this guarantee number (PG-2025-001234) and date. All documents must be dated, sealed and signed by Beneficiary.

**A3.** All dates on all documents must follow SWIFT format (YYMMDD) or ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD).

**A4.** Drawing exceeding this guarantee amount is not acceptable.

**A5.** All documents presented must be in strict compliance with the terms of this guarantee.

**A6.** All documents must be originally issued in English language.

**A7.** Partial shipments are not allowed.

**A8.** Transhipment is not allowed.

**A9.** Charter party B/L, stale B/L, short form blank back B/L, and non-negotiable seaway bills of lading are not acceptable.

**A10.** All original documents must be forwarded in one lot under one cover, quoting this guarantee reference number.

---

## CERTIFICATION

This Pool Guarantee Certificate is issued in digital format and authenticated through cryptographic signatures.
